name: Violet Corridor
summary: A lavender hallway lined with costume sketches that seem to shift at the edge of vision.
description: The hallway known as the Violet Corridor connects the parlor to the deeper parts of the house. The walls are painted a subdued lavender and lined with framed costume sketches from Lilith’s mortal career: gowns, masks, and stage designs drawn in careful pencil and ink. Under the soft glow of imitation gaslight sconces, the sketches take on a dreamlike quality. When viewed directly, each drawing appears ordinary, but glimpsed from the corner of the eye, some seem subtly changed—poses shifted, faces tilted, or details added that disappear when inspected again. The carpet is thick enough to deaden footsteps, so that conversations at either end of the corridor carry clearly while movement remains almost silent. Kindred with frayed nerves or heightened senses may experience the faint impression of whispers or distant applause here, especially when walking alone.
notes: Transitional space. Atmospheric effects should be subtle; major psychic effects remain at haven or oracle-chamber level.
owner_notes: Part of Lilith Nightshade’s private haven, the Violet Reliquary.
access_control: Interior access only; guests reach this room by invitation or escort.
security_notes: Perceptual unease and faint auditory impressions are narrative, not formal wards.
relationship_notes: Interior room of the Violet Reliquary, haven of Lilith Nightshade.
